enfoque_corto: Active isometrics level 1 + Ap/Yop Chagui + Koryo lines 1 and 3
enfoque: This cycle is focused on active isometrics level 1, technical kicking with Ap Chagui and Yop Chagui using the chair, and Koryo lines 1 and 3. The main goal is to improve clean technique, body alignment, wrist alignment, hip control, correct Yop Chagui trajectory, and smoother transitions. We will keep working little by little, with correct technique and trust in the practice already built in class and in the training plan.
chanonaflexDias: PRE: MONDAY TO SATURDAY / ADVANCED: MONDAY - WEDNESDAY - FRIDAY

chanonaflex.nota: Use this as general preparation before flexibility, isometrics, kicking, or poomsae work.
Advanced flexibility work to improve opening, control, mobility, and body alignment without forcing pain.

isometrico.nota: Keep the support leg strong, the hip aligned, and the return chamber clear.

tags: YOP CHAGUI, LEVEL 1, HIP CONTROL, TRAJECTORY
isometrico.nota: Keep the torso and waist aligned. Do not bow forward during the Yop Chagui.

isometrico.titulo: Glute and Hook - Active Isometric - Intermediate
isometrico.nota: Use this to strengthen the hip and glute for better stability in Yop Chagui and Koryo transitions.

pateoDias: TUESDAY - THURSDAY - SATURDAY / AP CHAGUI AND YOP CHAGUI WITH CHAIR

pateoTecnico.enfoque: Improve Ap Chagui with chair support, focusing on support leg, hip alignment, knee chamber, and controlled return.
Work on progressive height, knee chamber, extension, and return without losing body alignment.
pateoTecnico.reps: 8 to 10 reps per level / 1 to 2 sets

pateoTecnico.grupo: Yop Chagui with chair / base technique
pateoTecnico.enfoque: Bring the knee clearly toward the chest before extending the kick. Keep the torso and waist aligned without bowing forward.

pateoTecnico.titulo: Ap + Yop + Hip Turn with Chair
pateoTecnico.enfoque: Use the chair to work hip rotation, body axis, and the transition from Ap Chagui to Yop Chagui without losing waist alignment.

poomsaeDias: MONDAY TO SATURDAY / KORYO LINES 1 AND 3

poomsae.enfoque: Work on Koryo line 1 with clear preparation, correct posture, direction, movement cut, and timing.
Work rhythm, confidence, continuity, and technical timing in Koryo line 1.
Work on body alignment, head direction, hip control, and Yop Chagui execution on the right side.
Work on body alignment, head direction, hip control, and Yop Chagui execution on the left side.
Control timing, breathing, posture, and the clear final shape of the concentration movement.

apuntes: We are starting in a focused and important way with Yop Chagui and specific drills to improve technique, strength, and technical cleanliness.
This is a strong starting point because it helps us understand the muscles, alignment, and correct movement path in depth.
In the kicking section, work with control. Do not rush the kick or sacrifice technique for height.
The isometric exercises and ChanonaFlex routines are important, so please do them on the exact days marked in the plan.
For Yop Chagui, first build a clear knee chamber, then align the hip, and only after that extend the leg. Do not rush the kick.
If an exercise feels painful or something does not feel right, please tell me so we can adjust it safely.
Do not flex the wrists in the defenses. Keep the wrists in line with the forearm.
In Yop Chagui, keep the torso and waist aligned. Do not bow forward; this has been happening often and we need to correct it.
In the transitions, work on the correct encapsulation by turning with the ball of the foot.
When encapsulating, keep the legs almost together so the transition looks clean, compact, and well directed.
We want the movements to be fluid, but also technically correct according to what we have worked on in class and in this training plan.
Very important: work based on the technical details already seen in class and in the plan, trusting the practice little by little.
notasFinales: Let's keep giving it our best. We are improving little by little.
If you have any questions, please let me know.
